methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A defense and security technology company founded by Asaf Picciotto and former IDF special-forces veterans. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private-sector peers at the same or adjacent stage, supplemented by 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Top private comparables include NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median method applied across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a median valuation of $1.0B. The peer set ranges from $125M at the low end to $3.0B at the high end. Named sample peers—Dream Security ($3.0B Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B Series B), Code Metal ($1.2B Series B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (each $1.0B Series B)—anchor the distribution. This single triangulation method, though limited in methodological diversity, provides a clear central estimate.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $1.0B. Key uncertainties include SpectralX's specific revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and market differentiation—factors not yet quantifiable from available data. The reliance on a single valuation method constrains confidence; additional triangulation through revenue multiples or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen conviction. The peer range of $125M to $3.0B also reflects substantial dispersion, suggesting stage and sector positioning alone cannot fully resolve valuation.
